objective: Bootstrap the next issue-backed child under issue #615 for local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening on the bounded local hook and preflight surfaces only.
scope_boundary: Owns the local mutation-time pre-tool path only: .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts.
Must reference issue #617 as the already-closed startup/helper child rather than reopening its owned surfaces.

Keeps #607 as the planning-authority dependency, #612 as the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane, #614 as the downstream consumer verifier lane, and leaves broader hook-stack or CI rewrites for separate lanes.
Starts by producing the issue-local planning packet and validation evidence in this isolated worktree.
out_of_scope: Reopening issue #617 startup or helper surfacing work.
Planning-authority contract rewrites that belong to issue #607.
Execution-scope schema, fallback-log schema, or CI validator changes that belong to issue #612.
Downstream hldpro-sim consumer verifier or drift-gate work that belongs to issue #614.
Broad rewrites of hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, or scripts/orchestrator/delegation_gate.py.
Any claim of repo-wide write blocking closure.
research_summary: Parent issue #615 and merged child #617 leave a narrow residual slice: local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening for governance-surface writes. Live source inspection confirms the owned surfaces still have the relevant gaps: .claude/settings.json wires only Bash and Write pre-tool hooks, check_plan_preflight.py still defaults to .claude/plans rather than canonical docs/plans governed evidence, code-write-gate.sh still contains fail-open and warning-only branches on in-scope local mutation paths, and schema-guard.sh only blocks through the current narrow detection path. Specialist review confirmed #619 must stay on these local mutation surfaces only and must not reopen #617 or absorb #607, #612, or #614.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and execution-scope boundaries before local mutation-path changes.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, handoff, review packet, review placeholders, cross-review note, and validation artifact.
Restate the narrowed local mutation-only write surfaces confirmed by specialist review.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet is validated and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the exact local mutation-only owned surfaces.
The packet keeps #617, #607, #612, and #614 as explicit external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
The packet states that implementation is blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.
The packet requires a later per-surface proof matrix and bounded-write evidence rather than generic prose-only closure.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the narrowest valid owned slice under issue #615 after issue #617 merged.
specialist_reviews.summary: The narrowest valid issue #619 slice is mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening on .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts only.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the planning-only bootstrap packet shape, issue/branch alignment, and upfront AC and proof obligations.
specialist_reviews.summary: Use issue #617 as the nearest template, but keep issue #619 strictly planning_only on bootstrap with explicit owned and forbidden surfaces repeated across the PDCAR, structured plan, and handoff.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify what would make the lane invalid and what proof obligations the ACs must carry if the lane is truly bounded to local mutation-time pre-tool hardening.
specialist_reviews.summary: The lane becomes invalid if it reclaims #617 startup work, absorbs #607 or #612, broadens into general hook-stack cleanup, or claims repo-wide closure. The ACs must require concrete blocked and allowed transcripts with exact local-path proof.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review accepted the planning-only packet with no blocking findings and only two informational notes: specialist mixed-status resolution notes and future #614 forbidden-root alignment if that worktree is created.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #619 is authorized only for issue-local planning artifacts in this worktree until the packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate the planning packet, collect alternate-family review, and only then decide whether to promote issue #619 into an implementation-ready execution scope.
blocked_on: Issue #617 remains the already-closed startup/helper child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #607 remains the planning-authority dependency for the parent governance path.
Issue #612 remains the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the downstream hldpro-sim verifier or drift-gate lane.
Implementation is blocked until the issue-local planning packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #619 remains bounded to .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts only, and future implementation proof must include bounded-write evidence showing the actual diff stayed inside those owned surfaces and approved co-staging.
AC2: The planning packet explicitly preserves #617, #607, #612, and #614 as external boundaries rather than absorbing their owned surfaces or contracts.
AC3: No mutation-path implementation begins until the issue-local planning packet validates and the alternate-family review result is recorded.

AC4: Future implementation proof for issue #619 must require a per-surface proof matrix with concrete blocked and allowed local-path transcripts, including Edit or MultiEdit, named Bash mutation verbs, removed fail-open behavior, one compliant governance-surface allow case, and one read-only or out-of-scope allow case, each with exact command, tool path, exit code, and block or allow result.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit to hooks/pre-session-context.sh or scripts/overlord/check_execution_environment.py from issue #619 is a material deviation because those surfaces were already owned by issue #617.
Any edit to assert_execution_scope.py, fallback-log schema surfaces, or CI workflow enforcement from issue #619 is a material deviation because those belong to issue #612.
Any broad rewrite of hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, or scripts/orchestrator/delegation_gate.py from issue #619 is a material deviation unless the issue is rerouted.
Any implementation of local mutation-path behavior before the issue-local planning packet is validated and alternate-family reviewed is a material deviation.
Any claim that issue #619 closes repo-wide write blocking or fresh-session mis-routing is a material deviation.
